data_flow_pipeline.entry_point: User invokes bat command-line tool; main() in src/bin/bat/main.rs parses arguments via App::new() and clap, then calls run()

stages.responsibility: Parse command-line arguments, load configuration files, and build runtime configuration
stages.output: App struct with parsed arguments and Config struct with runtime settings

stages.responsibility: Open and read input files or stdin, handle preprocessing
stages.input: File paths or stdin from App.inputs()
stages.output: InputReader with opened file handles or piped data

stages.responsibility: Load syntax definitions, themes, and color schemes from cache or binary assets
stages.output: HighlightingAssets containing syntax sets and theme sets

stages.responsibility: Detect file syntax from filename/extension/content and select appropriate theme
stages.output: Selected Syntax and Theme for highlighting

stages.responsibility: Read lines from input, apply line range filters, and buffer for processing
stages.output: Buffered lines ready for syntax highlighting and decoration

stages.responsibility: Apply syntax highlighting, compute ANSI color codes, handle escape sequences
stages.input: Raw line content and Syntax definition
stages.output: Highlighted line with color regions and ANSI escape codes

stages.responsibility: Add line numbers, git diff markers, grid lines, handle tab expansion
Compute git diff when --diff flag is used
stages.input: File path and git repository context

stages.responsibility: Write formatted output to stdout or pager
stages.output: ANSI-escaped text written to stdout or piped to pager

data_flow_pipeline.exit_point: Formatted output is written to stdout via OutputHandle or piped to less pager; process exits with status code

main.rs.role: Application entry point; orchestrates argument parsing, config loading, and delegates to run_controller()

vscreen.rs.role: Virtual screen abstraction; manages ANSI escape sequences

theme.rs.role: Theme definition and color palette management

cross_module_interactions.interaction: main() calls App::new() to parse command-line arguments
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: ARGV and environment variables -> App struct
Config, inputs, and assets -> controller processes and prints

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Controller::print_file() creates InteractivePrinter and calls print_line()
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: InputReader, Config, HighlightingAssets -> formatted output lines
File name -> Syntax definition and Theme

cross_module_interactions.interaction: print_line() uses VirtualScreen for ANSI escape sequences

cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Line number, diff status, grid settings -> decorated output

cross_module_interactions.interaction: print_line() converts theme colors to ANSI codes
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Theme color definitions -> ANSI escape codes
File path -> HashMap of line changes

cross_module_interactions.interaction: print_line() writes formatted output via OutputHandle
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: ANSI-escaped formatted line -> stdout or pager
Pager configuration -> piped output to less

cross_module_interactions.interaction: EscapeSequenceIterator uses Style to parse escape sequences
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: ANSI escape sequences -> parsed style attributes

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Decorations use Style to apply colors

extension_proposal.feature: JSON export mode that outputs highlighted code as structured JSON with syntax tokens and metadata
required_changes: Modify printer.rs to support JSON serialization of highlighted regions
Extend output.rs to add OutputType::Json variant
Add new JsonPrinter implementing the Printer trait
Modify controller.rs to select JsonPrinter when JSON output is requested
integration_points: Stage 6 (Syntax Highlighting): JsonPrinter captures highlighted regions as structured data instead of ANSI codes
Stage 9 (Terminal Output): OutputType::Json writes JSON to stdout instead of ANSI-formatted text
Config: New output_format field determines printer selection
